mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320 index points, real borrowing costs compress equity valuations. This monetary tightening regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as discount rates rise and growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% approaching threshold, CPIAUCSL at 320.580
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite both conditions met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2%, near historical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1400 thousand units combined with increasing retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals by > 2% in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 103.5 index points signal supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.8799, slightly above threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23528.047 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand new jobs per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling labor market cooling that precedes broader economic slowdown and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100 thousand AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration without immediate unemployment spike
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction signal does not outperform PAYEMS-only signal by > 1.5% in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines below 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 318 index points, indicating monetary policy easing in a high-inflation regime that supports equity valuations through lower discount rates while inflation expectations remain anchored, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with moderating but elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 104.5 index points combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 160 thousand persons, signaling robust supply-side capacity expansion and labor productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with strong labor market support
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal fails to demonstrate superior predictive power (Rank IC difference < 1%) versus individual series.
